results.title: Sailing for beginners
results.brief_description: Instruction on dinghy sailing. Describes the main parts of a boat and her rigging, and explains and demonstrates various manoeuvres including going about, beating to windward, running, gybing, sailing triangular and figure of eight courses, and picking up a man overboard. Slow motion and diagrams are used. Filmed at Burnham-on-Crouch with the cooperation of the Royal Corinthian Yacht Club.

results.title: Safety oriented first aid. Unit 1
results.brief_description: One of a series of instructional first aid films. Concentrates on artificial respiration, with demonstrations of various methods, and illustrates causes of loss of breathing. Narrated by John Granik.

results.title: Rubella
results.creator_credit: Rubella Committee Deafness Foundation of Victoria
results.brief_description: Designed to inform pubescent and teenaged girls of the need to vaccinate against German measles to prevent rubella in the unborn child. Outlines the congenital defects caused by rubella in the first six weeks of pregnancy. Suitable for lower and middle secondary levels.

results.title: Rubber in today's world
results.brief_description: Reviews the history of rubber from its discovery to its use today as a basic commodity, including primitive methods of manufacture, improvements through vulcanisation, and modern production and process from plantation to market. Shows sources of and major steps in the production of synthetic rubber.

results.title: The Roundabout
results.brief_description: Explains that traffic can flow in 16 different directions at an ordinary intersection. The roundabout is designed to bring streams of traffic together, mix them and send them on their right way. Conflicting streams of traffic in a roundabout all travel in the same direction, and reduce head-on collisions.

results.title: Roots of plants
results.brief_description: Various forms of roots are first presented. Time-lapse and micro-photography are employed to bring out the interesting processes of growth and the structure of the roots of plants. Animation is used to demonstrate osmosis.
